codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of all 5 schema outputs (4A-4E) in json_export.py and CLI in eval.py. The code correctly handles numpy/pandas serialization via a dual approach (pre-sanitization + custom encoder), implements all argparse subcommands with proper required arg validation, and maintains Phase 1 backward compatibility (run.py untouched, 378 tests still green). 49 new tests provide comprehensive coverage of schema conformance, round-trip fidelity, type serialization, detection sorting, and cascade funnel ordering. A few non-blocking issues were identified.

issues.description: Schema 4B `params` field is always passed as empty dict `{}` in serialize_evaluation_run(). The Schema 4B spec shows params should contain actual parameter values (e.g., {"displacement": {"atr_multiplier": 1.5}}). The TODO comment says 'Could be enriched with actual params'. This means Phase 3 consumers reading params from Schema 4B will get no data. Non-blocking since detection results and stats are correct.

Redundant sanitization logic: RAJSONEncoder._sanitize() (line 95-122) and _deep_sanitize() (line 125-163) implement nearly identical recursive type conversion. write_json() calls _deep_sanitize before json.dump with RAJSONEncoder, so both run sequentially on the same data. The encoder's _sanitize is effectively dead code since _deep_sanitize already converts all numpy/pandas types. Not harmful but adds maintenance burden.

Double progress printing in cmd_sweep: _build_1d_grid() (line 240) already calls _print_progress for each step during metric computation, then cmd_sweep (line 177-178) loops over combo_metrics calling _print_progress again. The second loop replays progress after all work is done (no-op visually since it prints 100% immediately, but wasteful).

cmd_compare only runs locked baseline (single config). Despite docstring saying 'Compare two configs', it only passes {'current_locked': locked_results} to serialize_evaluation_run, meaning no pairwise comparison (Schema 4C) is ever generated. A second config (candidate) would need a separate --config2 arg or multi-config file. Feature description mentions 'Compare (--config, --data, --output)' so single-config may be intentional for Phase 2, but the naming is misleading.
Unused import: `import copy` in cmd_sweep is imported but never used.
Unused import: `import time` at module level is imported but never used.
Unused import: `extract_sweep_combos` from ra.evaluation.param_extraction is imported in cmd_sweep but never used.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The inline import pattern is used extensively in eval.py (imports inside function bodies like cmd_sweep, cmd_compare, cmd_walk_forward) for lazy loading. While pragmatic for CLI startup time, this pattern isn't documented in AGENTS.md coding conventions. Future workers may not follow it or may refactor it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: eval.py:119-124 (cmd_sweep), eval.py:300-306 (cmd_compare), eval.py:342-343 (cmd_walk_forward) — all use inline imports from ra.* modules.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Schema 4B params field is always empty when generated via serialize_evaluation_run. If Phase 3 depends on reading params from evaluation_run.json, it will get {}. This interface contract gap should be noted somewhere — either in output_schemas.md or AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/ra/output/json_export.py:380 — params={} comment says 'Could be enriched with actual params'. Spec at .factory/library/output_schemas.md Schema 4B shows params should contain actual values.

summary: PASS. The structured-output-and-cli feature is well-implemented with comprehensive test coverage (49 tests). All 5 JSON schemas (4A-4E) are correctly serialized with schema_version, numpy/pandas types are properly handled, detections are sorted by time, cascade funnel ordering is correct, and round-trip fidelity is verified. The eval.py CLI provides all 3 subcommands (sweep, compare, walk-forward) with proper --help and missing-arg error handling. Phase 1 run.py is unchanged and all 378 existing tests pass. Non-blocking issues found: empty params in Schema 4B, redundant sanitization code, double progress printing, unused imports, and compare subcommand only supporting single config. No blocking issues.
